ddls:
CREATE TABLE "database_engines" (
  "engine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each database engine */,
  "engine_name" TEXT /* Name of the database engine (e.g., Microsoft Jet) */,
  "engine_version" TEXT /* Version of the database engine */,
  "release_year" TEXT /* Year when the database engine was released */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Exact date when the database engine was released */,
  "end_of_life_date" TEXT /* Date when the database engine is no longer supported */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the database engine */,
  "patch_level" TEXT /* Latest patch level for the database engine */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product version */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the product version (e.g., 1.0, 1.1) */,
  "release_year" TEXT /* Year when the product version was released */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Exact date when the product version was released */,
  "end_of_life_date" TEXT /* Date when the product version is no longer supported */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the product version */,
  "patch_level" TEXT /* Latest patch level for the product version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_versions_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product (e.g., Microsoft Access) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the product */,
  "product_type" TEXT /* Type of the product (e.g., database, programming language) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Initial release date of the product */,
  "end_of_life_date" TEXT /* Date when the product is no longer supported */,
  "product_url" TEXT /* Link to the product's official documentation or website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_engine_compatibility" (
  "compatibility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compatibility record */,
  "engine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the database engine */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product version */,
  "compatibility_notes" TEXT /* Detailed notes on compatibility */,
  "compatibility_date" TEXT /* Date when the compatibility was verified */,
  "compatibility_status" TEXT /* Status of compatibility (e.g., fully compatible, partially compatible, not compatible) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compatibility_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_engine_compatibility_engine_id FOREIGN KEY ("engine_id") REFERENCES database_engines ("engine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_engine_compatibility_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES product_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_engine_compatibility_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "update_history" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product version */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description of the update */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the update was released */,
  "patch_level" TEXT /* Specific patch level of the update */,
  "update_url" TEXT /* Link to the update documentation */,
  "update_size" TEXT /* Size of the update file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_update_history_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES product_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "known_issues" (
  "issue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each known issue */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product version */,
  "issue_description" TEXT /* Description of the issue */,
  "issue_date" TEXT /* Date when the issue was identified */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution or workaround for the issue */,
  "issue_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the issue (e.g., critical, major, minor) */,
  "issue_type" TEXT /* Type of the issue (e.g., bug, performance, security) */,
  "issue_url" TEXT /* Link to more detailed information about the issue */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("issue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_known_issues_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES product_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, project manager, support staff) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the product */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the product information was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "access_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the access session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage record */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product version */,
  "usage_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the product version has been used */,
  "last_used_date" TEXT /* Date when the product version was last used */,
  "usage_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of usage (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "usage_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of usage */,
  "usage_environment" TEXT /* Environment where the product is used (e.g., development, production) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_usage_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES product_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collaboration" (
  "collaboration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration record */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team working on the product */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the collaboration */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the collaboration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the collaboration (e.g., ongoing, completed) */,
  "collaboration_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the collaboration */,
  "collaboration_objectives" TEXT /* Objectives of the collaboration */,
  "collaboration_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the collaboration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collaboration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaboration_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_collaboration_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "lead_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team lead */,
  "team_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the team */,
  "team_size" INTEGER /* Number of members in the team */,
  "team_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teams_lead_id FOREIGN KEY ("lead_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "team_members" (
  "member_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team member */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who is a member of the team */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the team member (e.g., developer, tester) */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the member joined the team */,
  "leave_date" TEXT /* Date when the member left the team */,
  "member_status" TEXT /* Current status of the member (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("member_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_team_members_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_team_members_team_id FOREIGN KEY ("team_id") REFERENCES teams ("team_id")
)
